solution.p: To simplify the expression 6\sqrt{7} \cdot 3\sqrt{{121}}, first simplify the square roots.
Next multiply all values not under the radical: 6\cdot3\cdot11={198}. So we have {198\sqrt{7}}.
Now that we have thought about how to simplify this expression, we can write the entire process as follows.

webwork-reps.pg:
# WeBWorK problem written by Danielle Rice # Portland Community College # # Multiplying radicals like a*sqrt(b)*c*sqrt(d). # Make d a square number. # # Last edited: Kling 8/17/13; Jordan 8/1/13; Carl Yao 7/22/13, Rice 7/9/13; Jordan 7/3/13 # # ENDDESCRIPTION
## Problem Tagging Information ## DBsubject('Algebra') ## DBchapter('Basic Algebra') ## DBsection('Exponents and Radicals') ## KEYWORDS('simplify','radical','square root') ## DBCCSS('8.EE') ## TitleText1('') ## EditionText1('') ## AuthorText1('') ## Section1('') ## Problem1('') ## Author('Danielle Rice, Alex Jordan, Carl Yao, Chris Hughes') ## Institution('PCC')
DOCUMENT();
loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "MathObjects.pl", "PGML.pl", "contextFraction.pl", "contextLimitedRadical.pl", "PCCmacros.pl", "PGcourse.pl", );
######################################
Context("Numeric"); $a = random(2,9,1); $b = random(2,9,1); $c = Real("$a*$b");
$d =list_random(2,5,11); $e =list_random(3,13,7); $g=Real($d*$d); $f = Real($g*$e); $i = Real($c*$d);
Context("LimitedRadical"); $cd = $c*$d; $h = Formula("$cd*sqrt($e)");
######################################
TEXT(beginproblem()); BEGIN_PGML
[@ PTX_exercisegroup_introduction('Simplify the expression.') @]
[@KeyboardInstructions("Use [|sqrt()|]* for the square root symbol. For example: [`\\sqrt{2}=`][|sqrt(2)|]*.")@]**
[`[$a]\sqrt{[$e]} \cdot [$b]\sqrt{[$g]} `]
[_]{$h}{16}
END_PGML
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION
To simplify the expression [`[$a]\sqrt{[$e]} \cdot [$b]\sqrt{[$g]}`], first simplify the square roots.
This gives [` [$a]\sqrt{[$e]} \cdot [$b]\cdot[$d]`].
Next multiply all values not under the radical: [`[$a]\cdot[$b]\cdot[$d]=[$i]`]. So we have [`[$h]`].
Now that we have thought about how to simplify this expression, we can write the entire process as follows.
[`` \begin{aligned} [$a]\sqrt{[$e]} \cdot [$b]\sqrt{[$g]}&=[$a]\sqrt{[$e]} \cdot [$b]\cdot[$d]\\ &=[$a]\cdot[$b]\cdot[$d]\sqrt{[$e]}\\ &=[$h]\\ \end{aligned} ``]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
######################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
